data.expose_coordinates: Expose GPS coordinates as entity attributes

data_description.zone_urls: Enter the URLs of the GeoJSON files that contain the zones you want to track. This supports both websites and files in the /config directory.
data_description.prioritize_zone_files: If you want to prioritize the order of the zone files, enable this option. This means that if a tracker is in multiple zones it will only consider those with the lowest priority.
data_description.download_zones: Use a local GeoJSON file to store the zones in. This will load the above defined files into a single file. The entities will only use this single file to retrieve the zones from. If no GeoJSON files are defined we will create an empty GeoJSON file.
data_description.registered_entities: Select the entities that you want to track in the zones.
data_description.expose_coordinates: When enabled, the mirror entity writes latitude, longitude, and gps_accuracy to its attributes on every update — Home Assistant's recorder then persists a full location history. Disable to publish only the zone name (privacy-first). New installs default to off.
data_description.allow_private_urls: ⚠️ Accept zone_urls that resolve to RFC-1918 private addresses (e.g. 192.168.x.x, 10.x.x.x, fd00::/8). Needed when the zone source lives on your LAN — typically the companion editor add-on. Loopback (127.0.0.1 / ::1), link-local (169.254.x, cloud metadata), multicast, and reserved ranges stay blocked regardless. Leave off unless you know you need it.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure Polygonal Zones
reconfigure.description: Update zone sources, tracked entities, and flags in place.

Loopback (127.0.0.1 / ::1), link-local (169.254.x, cloud metadata), multicast, and reserved ranges stay blocked regardless. Leave off unless you know you need it.

reload_zones.name: Reload Zones
reload_zones.description: Reload the zones stored in the GeoJSON files. This will retrieve them from the stored location and update the entities internal cache of the zones.

replace_all_zones.name: Replace All Zones
replace_all_zones.description: Replace all existing zones with new ones defined in GeoJSON format.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Polygonal Zones device whose zone file should be replaced.

zone.name: GeoJSON Data
zone.description: The GeoJSON FeatureCollection representing all zones to replace the existing ones.

add_new_zone.name: Add Zone
add_new_zone.description: Add a new zone to the existing zones.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Polygonal Zones device to add the zone to.

zone.name: Zone Data
zone.description: The GeoJSON Feature representing the new zone to add.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Polygonal Zones device whose zone should be edited.

zone_name.name: Zone Name
zone_name.description: The name of the zone to edit.

zone.name: Zone Data
zone.description: The GeoJSON Feature representing the modified zone.

delete_zone.name: Delete Zone
delete_zone.description: Remove a zone from the system.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The Polygonal Zones device whose zone should be deleted.

zone_name.name: Zone Name
zone_name.description: The name of the zone to delete.

zone_file_not_editable.message: The zone file for this device is read-only. Enable 'Download the GeoJSON files' in the integration options to make it editable.

zone_already_exists.message: A zone with that name already exists in this file.

zone_does_not_exist.message: No zone with that name exists in this file.

invalid_zone_data.message: The supplied zone data is invalid.

rate_limited.message: Zone mutation services on this entry are being called too fast. Wait a moment and try again.

zone_load_failed.title: Polygonal Zones could not load zones
zone_load_failed.description: The integration was unable to load the GeoJSON zone file for `{entity_id}` after several retry attempts. Check that the URL is reachable or the local path is correct, then call the `polygonal_zones.reload_zones` action or reload the integration.
